# Code of Conduct

`yoktez` is a small open-source project. The bar is low: be respectful, stay on-topic, and assume good faith.

## Standards

- Treat others as colleagues. Disagree with ideas, not people.
- Keep issues, pull requests, and discussions focused on the project — its API, behavior, documentation, and roadmap.
- Provide reproducible details (Python version, `yoktez` version, minimal example, full traceback) when reporting bugs. Vague reports get vague answers.
- Steer clear of harassment, personal attacks, discriminatory language, and content unrelated to the project.

## Enforcement

The maintainer reserves the right to edit, lock, or remove any contribution that violates these standards, and to block repeat or severe offenders from the project's GitHub presence.

## Reporting

Report conduct concerns privately to <hi@efe.cv>. Include links to the relevant comments, commits, or pull requests. Reports are handled in confidence; the reporter's identity is not shared without consent.

## Scope

This code of conduct applies to all project spaces — the GitHub repository, its issues and pull requests, and any official communication channel maintained on behalf of the project.
